title: CCP Visa Disclosure Act of 2025
stage_label: Referred to the House Committee on the Judiciary.
plain_summary: This bill makes new rules for people from other countries who want to come to the United States to study or participate in exchange programs. Anyone applying for a student or exchange visitor visa must tell the government if they get money from China's government or the Chinese Communist Party. If someone gets this money after they already have their visa, they must report it to the government right away. People who don't follow these reporting rules can have their visas taken away.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on the Judiciary.

sections.title: Section 1  Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Chinese Communist Party Visa Disclosure Act of 2025 or the CCP Visa Disclosure Act of 2025.

sections.title: Section 2  Disclosure on Certain Visa Applications
sections.text:
The Secretary of Homeland Security must update Form I-20 within one hundred eighty days after this law is passed. Form I-20 is the form that foreign students use to apply for student visas (called F visas and M visas). The updated form must ask students to report if they have received or plan to receive money from China's government or the Chinese Communist Party. Students must tell how much money they got and describe who gave them the money.

The Secretary of State must also update Form DS-2019 within one hundred eighty days after this law is passed. Form DS-2019 is used by people applying for exchange visitor visas (called J visas). These people must also report if they have received or plan to receive money from China's government or the Chinese Communist Party. They must tell how much money they got and describe who gave them the money.

If someone already has one of these visas and later receives money from China's government or the Chinese Communist Party, they must report it to both the Secretary of Homeland Security and the Secretary of State within ninety days. If they do not report this money, their visa can be taken away no matter when it was issued.

These reporting rules also apply to the spouse and minor children of anyone applying for or receiving these types of visas.

People who already have valid F, J, or M visas when this law is passed must report within one hundred eighty days if they have received or plan to receive money from China's government or the Chinese Communist Party. They must tell the Secretary of Homeland Security how much money they got and describe who gave them the money.

For this law, certain funds means any money that comes from China's government, the Chinese Communist Party, or any organization that is owned or controlled by China's government or the Chinese Communist Party.
